name: Chicken Fried Steak
recipeIngredient: 6 7-ounce portions of tenderized beef (either cube steak or tenderized round steak)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the meat with salt and pepper, set aside.
2. Beat the eggs and 1/2 cup of evaporated milk together in a bowl.
3. Heat the shortening in a heavy deep-sided skillet to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
4. Dredge the steaks in flour, dip them in the batter, then dredge again, shaking off excess.
5. When the fat is hot, add the steaks a few at a time, being careful not to overcrowd the pan.
6. Fry for 4 minutes on one side until golden brown, turn with tongs or a slotted spatula and fry the other side.
7. Drain on paper towels and keep warm in an oven set to 150 degrees Fahrenheit.
8. To make the gravy, pour all but 4 tablespoons of fat from the skillet and return heat to low.
9. Add the flour and cook over low heat for 3 minutes, stirring constantly; do not let it burn.
10. Add the remaining evaporated milk, whisking until smooth and scraping the bottom of the pan to retrieve small bits clinging to it.
11. Gradually add water, stirring constantly until the gravy is smooth and of desired thickness.
12. Add salt and pepper to taste and pour into a serving bowl.
Note:
The same basics used for gravy are also very good with fried chicken.
